LegalDisclaimer.title: Please confirm before proceeding
LegalDisclaimer.text: To maintain the integrity of our community and the spirit of fair use, we ask you to confirm two important points:
LegalDisclaimer.ticket: I own a valid ticket for my journey.
LegalDisclaimer.ticketDescription: I understand that this app is designed to improve awareness and planning in public transport, not to circumvent rules or regulations.
LegalDisclaimer.activeUsage: I am not actively using the app during my journey.
LegalDisclaimer.activeUsageDescription: I understand that the active use of the app during my journey can disturb other passengers and violate the terms of use of the public transport operator.
LegalDisclaimer.saved: Your confirmation will be saved to your browser's local storage.

ContactSection.title: Contact us
ContactSection.about-us-text: FreiFahren is a non-commercial open-source project aimed at making public transport more accessible. We are always open to new contributors on our
ContactSection.telegram-group-info: Most of the reports come from the FreiFahren_BE Telegram group. Thank you to the admins for allowing us to extract the relevant information with our Telegram bot.

contact.title: Contact
contact.form: Additional electronic contact channel: in-app contact form.

preamble.title: 1. Preamble
preamble.content: This Privacy Policy informs you about the nature, scope, and purpose of the processing of personal data in the mobile application "Freifahren" (hereinafter referred to as "App"). The App allows users to report incidents in public transport and share this information with other users. Additionally, reports are generated by the non-deterministic and anonymized evaluation of messages from the Freifahren_BE Telegram group.

responsible.title: 2. Responsible Party
responsible.content: The controller responsible for data processing within this App is FreiFahren e.V. - Johan Trieloff (Chairman), c/o Online-Impressum #7591, Europaring 90, 53757 Sankt Augustin, reachable via contact@freifahren.org. A second electronic contact channel is available via the in-app contact form. Authorized board members: Johan Trieloff (johan@freifahren.org), Moritz Clerc (moritz@freifahren.org), David Brandes (david@freifahren.org).

legalBasis.title: 3. Legal Basis for Data Processing
legalBasis.content: The processing of personal data in our App is based on the following legal grounds in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR):
legalBasis.consent: Consent (Art. 6 para. 1 lit. a GDPR): Users of our App provide their explicit consent for the processing of their personal data for specific purposes. This consent can be revoked at any time, whereby the revocation does not affect the legality of the processing carried out up to that point.
legalBasis.contract: Contractual performance and pre-contractual measures (Art. 6 para. 1 lit. b GDPR): Processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is a party or to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract.
legalBasis.legitimateInterest: Legitimate interests (Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR): Processing is necessary to protect the legitimate interests of the controller or a third party, provided these interests are not overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject.

dataUsage.title: 4. Data Collection and Usage
dataUsage.content: The App collects data through two different forms:
dataUsage.reportPurpose: The reported data is temporarily made available to other users of the App to provide them with information about the current situation in public transport. Additionally, the reports are forwarded to the Freifahren_BE Telegram group. This data transmission serves the purpose of sharing and informing the community about incidents.
dataUsage.feedbackPurpose: The feedback is used exclusively for improving the App and is not shared with third parties or in the Telegram group.
dataUsage.ipAddressInfo: The IP address is used to detect spam and abuse reports to ensure that the submissions come from real users and not from bots or other automated sources. IP addresses are stored in heavily encrypted form for a maximum of {{ minutes }} minutes and then deleted. They are not linked to other data and cannot be traced back to the user.

anonymity.title: 5. Anonymity
anonymity.content: No permanently personal data of users is stored. Reports are anonymous, and no conclusions can be drawn about individual persons. The IP address is considered personal data, but it is not stored persistently and is not reconstructable.

storageAndAccess.title: 6. Storage and Access
storageAndAccess.content: Collected data is stored on FreiFahren e.V.'s own servers, hosted by Hetzner Online GmbH in Germany. Access is strictly limited to authorized association members. Reports and feedback are stored without an automatic deletion period. Timestamps older than 4 hours are stored in rounded form to preserve the anonymity of reports. In addition, data is secured in regular backups (currently daily). Long-term storage is based on the legitimate interests of FreiFahren e.V. in accordance with Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR and serves the following purposes: Operational security and abuse prevention: Historical report data enables the detection of manipulation patterns and spam waves as well as the continuous improvement of countermeasures. Quality improvement: The data basis is used to calibrate and validate forecasting and risk logic. Longer-term time series are required for this to capture seasonality, event peaks, and holiday periods. Aggregated evaluations: Long-term aggregated usage patterns support the technical and content-related further development of the app. To minimize data protection risk, no real names or user accounts are stored, timestamps are rounded after 4 hours, and data access is subject to strict role and access management as well as technical and organizational safeguards.

userRights.title: 7. User Rights
userRights.content: Users have the right to request information about their personal data, as well as to request the correction or deletion of such data. Requests can be submitted via the App's contact form or directly by email to contact@freifahren.org.

consent.title: 8. Consent to the Privacy Policy
consent.content: Users must agree to this Privacy Policy before submitting a report in the App. This consent is a prerequisite for using the App.

dataProtectionOfficer.title: 9. Data Protection Officer
dataProtectionOfficer.content: For questions regarding data protection, please contact us via contact@freifahren.org.

policyChanges.title: 10. Changes to the Privacy Policy
policyChanges.content: We reserve the right to change this Privacy Policy to reflect changes in legal conditions, the App, or data processing. Users are encouraged to regularly review the content of the Privacy Policy.

telegramMessages.title: 11. Processing of Telegram Messages
telegramMessages.content: Messages from the Freifahren_BE group are evaluated by the Freifahren Telegram Bot with the consent of the administrators. Messages are not stored and are only used for real-time evaluation. Only the non-deterministically extracted information (direction, line, and station) from the messages is stored. The timestamp is rounded to ensure anonymity.

errorMonitoring.title: 12. Error Monitoring and Diagnostics
errorMonitoring.description: We use Sentry for error monitoring and crash reporting to maintain and improve our service. When the app encounters an error, we collect anonymized diagnostic information including:

dataPoints.state: App state at the time of the error

errorMonitoring.storage: This technical data is stored on Sentry's infrastructure located in the European Union for a maximum period of 30 days. We explicitly configure Sentry to not collect IP addresses. All data collection is anonymized and used solely for the purpose of identifying and fixing technical issues.

dataProcessing.title: 13. Data Processing by Service Providers
dataProcessing.content: Where external service providers are used in the context of data processing, they act as processors within the meaning of Art. 28 GDPR. A data processing agreement (Data Processing Addendum) is in place with Sentry (Functional Software, Inc.). Corresponding data processing agreements with Hetzner Online GmbH are being concluded or are in preparation.

severabilityClause.title: 14. Severability Clause
severabilityClause.content: If any provision of this Privacy Policy is found to be wholly or partially invalid or unenforceable, or if it subsequently becomes invalid or unenforceable, the validity of the remaining provisions shall not be affected.

FeedbackSummaryModal.title: Thank you for your feedback!
FeedbackSummaryModal.description: Would you like to join our Telegram feedback group to help us improve FreiFahren further? Your feedback can be read directly by the developers of the app.

MarketingModal.title: FreiFahren Mobile App
MarketingModal.subtitle: Real-time updates on the go
MarketingModal.description: Get real-time updates about reports on your mobile device. Never be caught off guard again!
